title: From Fitness To Software The Brotein Arc
html:
The project genealogy on the surface is BroMenu → Brotein → Rankett. The interesting question is why those three and in that order, and the answer is not "Yahir got bored of fitness and got interested in AI search." The answer is that all three are the same wedge — use LLMs to dissolve a slow human bottleneck — applied to progressively higher-margin surface area, and fitness was the seed because fitness was the thing Yahir was already paying attention to. This page traces the thinking, not just the timeline.

The through-line

If you compress the genealogy to one sentence: Yahir kept the founder filter constant (asking the right question + nonobvious + use-LLMs-to-remove-a-human-bottleneck) and rotated the surface area until he found one with agency-channel distribution and high LTV. Fitness was where he started because his own life — ISSA personal trainer cert, the supplement stack in 2024 08 14 travel occurrences, the overnight gym shift in 2025 09 23 how im scraping by — was already pre-loaded with domain context. AI-search visibility for marketing agencies is where he ended up because the same wedge applied there has 10x the contract value and a built-in distribution layer (whitelabel strategy through agencies). The pivot is not a topical shift; it is a channel discovery.

The seed: 2024-12-21, the BroMenu PRD

2024 12 21 todos is the first real product spec in the corpus. Written one week after 2024 12 14 departing china, it defines BroMenu as "a fitness-focused iOS mobile app for identifying the most anabolic menu items from restaurant menus, helping users achieve muscle building, bulking, or cutting goals." The stack is named — swiftui + windsurf ide, openai api for OCR + GPT for anabolic scoring, supabase for storage + Sign in with Apple — and the UX inspiration is explicitly alipay / wechat QR-scan flows. Two things to notice:

First, the wedge is already correctly framed: the bottleneck is "what should I eat at this restaurant if I'm trying to bulk/cut" and the human normally has to scan the menu, do the macro math, and decide. BroMenu collapses that into a camera shot. This is the mindless human interface disruption template from 2024 05 13 things applied to a tiny daily decision. Same wedge as Rankett (collapse a slow human pattern-matching task into an LLM call), different surface area.

Second, the no runway discipline is already running through the technical decisions: "avoid paying the apple developer fee," "Reduce API call costs by caching responses wherever possible." The cost-vigilance precedes the 2025 09 23 how im scraping by inventory by nine months — it is a constant, not a response.

Why fitness specifically — domain context as wedge selection

Yahir didn't pick fitness because the market was big. He picked it because he was already in the data. The supplement stack tail of 2024 08 14 travel occurrences (magnesium, fish oils, D3, BCAA, creatine) is him personally tracking inputs. The ISSA cert he was paying for in mid-2025 (per Memory) is him formalizing the credential that makes the gym shift hireable. The markers of development checklist from 2024 08 31 determinants of advanced countries includes "do they prioritize lifting/fitness?" as a country-evaluation axis. Fitness was already the lens he was seeing through — picking it as the first product wedge is the lowest-friction starting point for a solo founder. The lesson generalizes: domain context is itself a moat for a solo person, because it lets you build without doing customer-discovery from cold.

The mid-2025 build sprint

By mid-2025 the spec is in execution. 2025 07 09 screenshot 2025 07 09 at 4 51 29 pm 720cb8bf is the supabase menu_items table mid-seeding — id, restaurant_id, name, description, ~40 rows across Chick fil A, McDonald's, Starbucks, Subway. The seeding strategy is revealing: items aren't being loaded by full restaurant catalog, they're being loaded by anabolic merit — protein boxes, egg bites, McMuffins, carved turkey. Yahir is bootstrapping the dataset top-down by what the anabolic scoring axis would surface as good picks. Build-the-pipe-then-fill-it move.

Eight days later the nutrition-scrape sprint kicks off in a 4am burst on July 17–18: Chipotle (the proof case — bowl assembly is where anabolic-scoring matters most), Little Caesars (the negative example — pizza chains will all score badly), Wingstop (the gold standard — 10g protein per 90-cal wing), Taco Bell ×3 (the SKU sprawl problem — 30+ drink variants). The sequence shows Yahir thinking about coverage: one positive case, one negative, one with hard SKU disambiguation, one with bowl-assembly semantics. He is bootstrapping a fast food nutrition dataset with the same instinct a research scientist has when sampling — span the conditions, not the catalog.

What broke — or what didn't break, but didn't scale

By the time 2025 09 23 how im scraping by is written in late September, brotein is no longer the headline project of the corpus. Memory frames rankett as "all my energy and time and future banking right now" and the Brotein project page asks the open question outright: "Is it still active in 2026 or did it get sunsetted in favor of Rankett?" The corpus does not contain a death certificate, but the absence is itself the answer. The pivot is real and the pivot was rational.

Why? Three structural reasons the corpus supports:

(1) LTV ceiling. A consumer fitness app monetizes at $5–$15/mo per user with high churn. The Rankett GTM bible sells at $998/seat/month to agencies who resell at $3K–$6K/mo to clients. Same wedge, 60–600x the per-customer revenue.

(2) Distribution. BroMenu is direct-to-consumer in the App Store — the most expensive distribution surface in software. Rankett is sold through agencies only — leveraged distribution where the agency's existing client book becomes the funnel. The whitelabel strategy insight is the actual unlock.

(3) Filter quality. Consumer apps can't filter customers. Agencies can be filtered with the 60 day activation rule and the 5+ existing clients requirement. The filter buys back Yahir's most scarce resource — solo-founder attention — by removing tire-kickers structurally instead of through customer-service triage.

The wedge stays constant

What's striking is how little the wedge changes between BroMenu and Rankett. BroMenu uses an LLM to remove the "look at menu, do macro math" human task. Rankett uses LLMs to remove the "manually optimize content for AI search engines" agency task. Both are mindless human interface disruption. Both compress a slow pattern-matching task into a fast LLM-mediated one. Both came out of the same 2022 asking the right question founder filter recorded in 2022 10 25 ahmad jamal stream. The pivot is from a low-LTV consumer surface with no distribution to a high-LTV B2B2B surface with white-label distribution — but it is the same product instinct, applied differently.

The surprise

The surprising find is that fitness was a tactical wedge, not a passion. The corpus reads — at first glance — like Yahir is "into fitness and built a fitness app." But triangulate the supplement stack, the issa cpt credential as a gym-shift hireable, the markers of development "do they prioritize lifting" country-evaluation question, and the cost-discipline framing throughout 2024 12 21 todos, and a different picture emerges: fitness was the lowest-friction domain Yahir could use to learn the LLM-app build stack, with the issa cpt credential simultaneously serving as bridge-income insurance. The actual passion is the wedge itself — "asking the right question" + "remove the mindless human interface" — not the topic. When the topic stopped scaling, Yahir kept the wedge and changed the topic, which is exactly what someone whose love-object is the pattern and not the subject would do. Rankett is BroMenu with a better channel; the love-object never moved.

A second smaller surprise: the 4am nutrition-scrape sprint is a perfect specimen of the late night writing patterns financial-reckoning genre even though it's about fitness data, not money. The 4:56:13 → 4:56:23 → 4:56:30 timestamp triple — three Taco Bell screenshots in 17 seconds — is a founder grinding solo at 5am because there is no team and no one is going to do the grunt work for him. The Brotein arc and the founder-loneliness arc are the same arc.

Open questions

  • Did brotein ever ship publicly? "Brotein to Market" was named as a goal on 6/30/2025 but the corpus has no launch artifact.
  • Is the supabase menu_items data still in production, archived, or wiped? It's the most expensive asset of the BroMenu era — was any of it transferable to Rankett's pooled data network effect backend?
  • The category — are any of those Brotein-adjacent (fitness, nutrition, anabolic-scoring) or are they all already in the ai search optimization orbit?
  • Does Yahir still use the BroMenu camera prototype himself? It's the kind of artifact founders keep using personally even after sunset.
